checks.label: All expected /orbit source files exist.
Page uses a stable viewport shell with internal scroll.
Demo navigation wraps and keeps touch-height links.
Orbit components use min-w-0 guards in dense panels.
Orbit components use flex-wrap for dense controls.
Orbit components use breakpoint grids instead of fixed desktop-only columns.
Permission matrix collapses before the five-column desktop layout.
Vision bridge uses responsive cards for the pipeline tracks.
Installation topology uses responsive cards for the office system map.
Health readiness uses responsive cards for local telemetry channels.
Risk register uses responsive cards for approval gates.
Command contract uses responsive cards for command intents.
Audit trail uses responsive cards for trace events.
KosmoDesign handoff panel uses responsive columns for role, model, blockers and context.
Office routine uses responsive cards for day phases and hard stops.
Runtime contract uses responsive cards for the runtime stages.
Workstation profile contract uses responsive cards for profile and escalation layouts.
Local identity contract uses responsive cards for profile classes, sessions and promotion requirements.
Data governance contract uses responsive cards for domains, storage lanes and promotion requirements.
Progress map uses stable bar height and constrained width.
Demo readiness summary uses responsive columns.
Publish readiness uses responsive columns for live gate statuses.
Workflow delta uses a responsive comparison grid.
Pilot measurement uses responsive metric and rule grids.
Pilot runbook uses responsive cards for timed steps, evidence and hard stops.
Pilot session template uses responsive columns for session and metric cards.
Learning mode uses responsive cards for learning profiles and tracks.
Long labels can break instead of overflowing pills.
No /orbit source scales font size directly with viewport width.
No /orbit source uses negative letter spacing.

next_actions: Use this as a source-level guard before the real browser/mobile smoke.
Do not treat this as a replacement for a visual browser check.
